preview.summary: World Cup debutants Curacao, the smallest nation ever to qualify, face the athletic, experienced Elephants in a pivotal Group E clash. Ivory Coast's pace and physicality test Dick Advocaat's well-drilled, tournament-newcomer underdogs.
preview.homeForm: Topped their CONCACAF group unbeaten on debut; climbed to 82nd in the FIFA rankings, the smallest nation ever to qualify.
preview.awayForm: Unbeaten CAF Group F winners with the best goal difference in African qualifying; ranked 34th by FIFA.
preview.homeKey: Rangelo Janga, all-time top scorer leading the front line.
preview.awayKey: Sébastien Haller, physical focal point of the attack.
storylines: Curacao's historic first World Cup appearance under Dick Advocaat
Ivory Coast's athletic transitions against a compact debutant
Group E knockout-round hopes likely hinge on this result
preview.h2h: The sides have no notable prior meetings, making this a first competitive encounter.
